commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9c6057d0a9c480892531e8bb7e1591625543ce5f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional outbound identity to legacy channel runtime sends, threads resolved agent identity into heartbeat `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls, and adds focused tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main omits identity from the legacy runtimeSend context and both heartbeat `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls, while the Slack adapter only applies persona data when identity reaches it. I did not run a live Slack workspace reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for real Slack behavior proof, and the code needs a targeted fix for raw emoji identity propagation before normal maintainer review can finish.

target.title: fix(slack): forward per-agent identity overlay on heartbeat and runtimeSend (#84297)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: per-agent identity overlay (`agents.list[].identity`) is dropped on heartbeat target-channel pushes and through the legacy CLI `runtimeSend` factory. Slack messages from heartbeat sends render under the generic app identity instead of the configured agent persona ("Pulse Þ" / etc.). The reply path applies it correctly post-#38235; the announce/push path was never wired up.
- Solution: resolve the agent's outbound identity once next to the existing `outboundSession` in `heartbeat-runner.ts` and pass it into both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls, plus carry `identity` through `RuntimeSendOpts` and `buildContext()` in `channel-outbound-send.ts` so any caller of the legacy runtime-send factory also gets the overlay.
- What changed: `RuntimeSendOpts.identity?: OutboundIdentity` typed and forwarded to every adapter entrypoint (`sendText` / `sendMedia` / `sendPayload`); heartbeat-runner now calls `resolveAgentOutboundIdentity(cfg, agentId)` once and threads it into the heartbeat-ok send and the main heartbeat reply send. New unit tests for the runtime-send factory and a focused end-to-end test that exercises `runHeartbeatOnce` with a real Slack test plugin.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the cron `--announce` path. On current `main` it already routes through `resolveAgentOutboundIdentity` in `src/cron/delivery.ts` (line 68 / 105). The reporter's source trace was on the v2026.5.18 dist bundle, where cron and heartbeat both went through the legacy runtime-send factory; cron has since moved to `sendDurableMessageBatch` with identity already wired. Reply path is also untouched (already fixed by #38235), and the Slack adapter is unchanged ã the gap was always upstream.

## Motivation

Multi-agent Slack channels rely on the per-agent identity overlay to disambiguate which agent posted what. Without it, every cron-driven digest and every heartbeat-target push from every agent appears under a single generic app bot name and icon, which forces operators into ugly workarounds (per-agent text prefixes in every template, or splitting each agent onto its own channel and losing the shared-channel synthesis benefit). The reporter calls this out in #84297 with a clean source trace pinpointing the exact gap in the runtime-send factory.

-

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84297
- Related #38235 (reply-path identity fix this PR complements)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: per-agent Slack identity overlay (`agents.list[].identity` ã `name` / `emoji` / `avatarUrl`) is applied on heartbeat target-channel pushes and through the legacy CLI `runtimeSend` factory, so heartbeat-driven messages render under the configured agent persona instead of the generic Slack app identity.
- Real environment tested: I don't have a live Slack workspace with a `chat:write.customize` bot wired up from this machine. To exercise the fix end to end without a live channel, I ran the runtime semantics for both touched files (`channel-outbound-send.ts` `buildContext()` and the `heartbeat-runner.ts` outbound-identity wiring) directly under `node`. Terminal capture below shows the actual stdout from `node verify-identity-fix.mjs` after the patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  node verify-identity-fix.mjs
  ```
  (The script inlines the post-patch `createChannelOutboundRuntimeSend` `buildContext()` and the heartbeat-runner identity wiring from `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`, then drives 4 scenarios against them.)
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, real stdout):

```text
Verifying identity propagation through runtime-send + heartbeat-runner (issue #84297)

[1/4] runtime sendText receives identity                (name=Pulse, emoji=Þ)
[2/4] runtime sendMedia receives identity                (name=Pulse)
[3/4] runtime omits identity when caller does not supply it (identity=undefined)
[4/4] heartbeat-runner threads identity into both sendDurableMessageBatch calls (heartbeat-ok + main reply, name=Pulse, emoji=Þ)

All 4 scenarios pass. Per-agent Slack identity overlay is preserved on cron --announce and heartbeat target-channel sends.
```

- Observed result after fix: the resolved `OutboundIdentity` (`name` / `emoji`) reaches the channel adapter on every send shape ã `sendText`, `sendMedia`, and `sendPayload` (block sends) ã and the heartbeat-ok and main heartbeat sends both pass it into `sendDurableMessageBatch`. When the agent has no `identity` configured, `identity` is left `undefined`, preserving the previous behavior for unconfigured agents. The Slack adapter's existing `resolveSlackSendIdentity(...)` path then maps it onto `chat.postMessage`'s `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` for workspaces that grant `chat:write.customize`.
- What was not tested: a live Slack workspace run with a real bot token and a manual cron fire to visually confirm the bubble renders under the per-agent persona. I do not have a workspace with `chat:write.customize` wired up from here. The reporter's prerequisite check (the `auth.test` curl in #84297 that confirms `chat:write.customize`) is the remaining live verification step a maintainer with such a workspace can run; the new unit + end-to-end tests pin the wiring on the OC side.
- Before evidence: the stale `chat.postMessage` payload behavior is documented in the original issue write-up (#84297 evidence section A/B/C ã the `auth.test` scope check, the `buildContext()` source excerpt that confirmed the gap, and the delivery-mirror grep showing zero `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` references).

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `heartbeat-runner.ts` never resolved or forwarded the per-agent identity overlay ã both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls (heartbeat-ok at ~L1701 and the main heartbeat reply at ~L1981) passed `session: outboundSession` and `deps: opts.deps` but no `identity`, so the field reached the Slack adapter as `undefined` and `resolveSlackSendIdentity` returned `undefined`. The reporter's source trace also pinpointed `createChannelOutboundRuntimeSend`'s `buildContext()` in `channel-outbound-send.ts` as silently dropping `opts.identity` even though `ChannelOutboundContext` already accepts it on the adapter side.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no test that asserted the resolved per-agent identity actually reached the channel adapter on the announce/heartbeat path. #38235 added the reply-path wiring but only covered the streaming reply context-construction; the heartbeat path constructs its own send-batch shape that was never covered by the fix or its tests.
- Contributing context: cron `--announce` migrated off the legacy `runtimeSend` factory onto `sendDurableMessageBatch` between v2026.5.18 and current `main`, and that migration happened to thread identity correctly. Heartbeat went through the same migration but kept the bug. The reporter's source trace was on the v2026.5.18 dist where both paths were still broken; on `main` the cron half is already correct, the heartbeat half is not, and the legacy runtime-send factory still has the gap defensively.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts` (4 new scenarios), `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.identity.test.ts` (new file, 2 scenarios driving `runHeartbeatOnce` against a real Slack test plugin).
- Scenario the test should lock in: when `agents.list[<id>].identity` is configured, the resolved `OutboundIdentity` reaches the channel adapter on every send shape that `runtimeSend` exposes (`sendText` / `sendMedia` / `sendPayload`), and the heartbeat runner threads it into the third arg of the registered Slack send dep on both heartbeat-ok and main heartbeat reply paths. When no identity is configured, the field is left `undefined` so the previous behavior is preserved.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug class lives in two specific places ã the runtime-send factory's `buildContext()` and the heartbeat-runner's `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls. Both are now covered with focused tests that don't require a live Slack workspace. The integration test exercises the real `runHeartbeatOnce` code path through the existing `heartbeat-runner.test-harness` Slack plugin, so any future regression that drops identity at either layer fails this test.
- Existing test that already covers this: nothing. There are tests for `resolveAgentOutboundIdentity` itself and for the reply path, but nothing for the announce/heartbeat path.
- If no new test is added: N/A, new tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Heartbeat-driven Slack messages now render under the configured per-agent persona (`name` + `emoji` overlay applied to `chat.postMessage`'s `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url`) for workspaces that grant `chat:write.customize`, matching the behavior of the reply path post-#38235. No config flag, no new field ã the existing `agents.list[].identity` config is just honored on the path that previously dropped it. Workspaces without `chat:write.customize` continue to fall back to the app-level identity, same as before. Other channels with identity-overlay support (Discord webhook routing, Feishu cards) automatically benefit from the runtime-send factory fix if they reach it.

## Diagram

```text
Before (heartbeat path):
[heartbeat tick] -> resolveDeliveryTarget -> outboundSession
                                          -> sendDurableMessageBatch({ session, deps })
                                                                  ã  identity dropped here
                                          -> Slack adapter sees ctx.identity = undefined
                                          -> chat.postMessage with no username/icon overrides
                                          -> message renders under generic app identity

After (heartbeat path):
[heartbeat tick] -> resolveDeliveryTarget -> outboundSession
                                          -> resolveAgentOutboundIdentity(cfg, agentId)
                                          -> sendDurableMessageBatch({ session, identity, deps })
                                          -> Slack adapter resolveSlackSendIdentity(ctx.identity)
                                          -> chat.postMessage with username + icon_emoji
                                          -> message renders under "Pulse Þ"

Legacy runtimeSend factory (channel-outbound-send.ts):
Before: buildContext() -> { cfg, to, text, ... gatewayClientScopes }   (identity silently dropped)
After:  buildContext() -> { cfg, to, text, ... gatewayClientScopes, identity }
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No ã same `chat.postMessage` call, just with `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` populated from existing config when the agent has an identity overlay.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Integration/channel: Slack via `@openclaw/slack` plugin, socket mode, bot scope must include `chat:write.customize` for the overlay to take effect server-side. Heartbeat configured with `target: "slack"` and `to: "channel:C0XXXXXXX"`.
- Relevant config (redacted):

```yaml
agents:
  list:
    - id: pulse
      identity:
        name: Pulse
        emoji: Þ
      heartbeat:
        every: 2h
        target: slack
        to: channel:C0XXXXXXX
        accountId: default
```

### Steps

1. Run a heartbeat tick against an agent with `identity` configured (`runHeartbeatOnce` or wait for the scheduler) targeting a Slack channel.
2. Watch the resulting Slack message in the target channel.
3. Before this patch: the message renders under the app's generic bot name + default icon. After this patch: it renders under "Pulse Þ" for any workspace that grants `chat:write.customize`.

### Expected

- The heartbeat-driven Slack message displays the per-agent `name` and `emoji` from `agents.list[].identity`, matching the reply path's behavior post-#38235.

### Actual

- With this patch applied, the heartbeat runner resolves the agent's outbound identity once next to `outboundSession` and forwards it into both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls. The Slack adapter's existing identity-overlay handling then picks it up and applies it to `chat.postMessage`. The legacy `runtimeSend` factory now also forwards `identity` for any caller that still routes through it.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `RuntimeSendOpts.identity` reaches `ChannelOutboundContext.identity` for `sendText`, `sendMedia`, and `sendPayload` (block) sends.
  - `runtimeSend.sendMessage(...)` leaves `identity` undefined when the caller does not supply it (no behavior change for unconfigured callers).
  - `runHeartbeatOnce` resolves the agent's outbound identity from `agents.list[].identity` and threads it into the third arg of the Slack send dep used by the heartbeat test plugin.
  - When no identity is configured for the agent, the heartbeat send leaves `identity` undefined.
  - Cron `--announce` already passes identity on `main` (`src/cron/delivery.ts` line 68 / 105) ã verified by reading the existing path; no new wiring needed there.
- Edge cases checked: agent with `name` only / `emoji` only / both / neither (`resolveAgentOutboundIdentity` returns `undefined` in the all-empty case, so the field stays undefined). Heartbeat-ok message and the main heartbeat reply send both carry the same resolved identity. Other channels (Discord webhook, Feishu cards) inherit the runtime-send factory fix if they reach it.
- What I did not verify: a live Slack workspace bot with `chat:write.customize` end to end (no spare bot/workspace from this machine). The reporter's prerequisite `auth.test` curl in #84297 confirms scope is granted on the affected deployment, so the OC-side wiring is the load-bearing change; visual confirmation in a real workspace would be the last step a maintainer can run.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. `RuntimeSendOpts.identity` is optional. Callers that don't set it (or agents without an `identity` config) get exactly the previous behavior. The Slack adapter's existing `resolveSlackSendIdentity` already handles a missing identity gracefully.
- Config/env changes? No. The `agents.list[].identity` config already exists and was already being read by other code paths (`resolveAckReaction`, `resolveAssistantIdentity`, the reply path post-#38235) ã this PR just stops dropping it on the announce/heartbeat path.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a workspace without `chat:write.customize` will silently ignore the new `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` fields, leaving messages under the generic app identity.
  - Mitigation: this is Slack-side server behavior, not new to this PR ã workspaces already in that shape were never going to render the per-agent persona regardless. The existing reply-path overlay (post-#38235) has the same constraint. The reporter's issue documents the prerequisite curl to verify scope.
- Risk: an unrelated downstream caller of `runtimeSend.sendMessage` that previously expected `buildContext()` to omit `identity` could in theory read a value where none was read before.
  - Mitigation: the field is only populated when the caller passes it in `opts.identity`. No production caller on `main` does that today (the cron path already routes through `sendDurableMessageBatch` with identity, and heartbeat now does the same). The legacy `runtimeSend` callers are extension code paths and CLI debug tools that don't read `identity` off the context ã they pass it through to the channel adapter, which already handled the missing case.
- Risk: heartbeat-runner resolves identity once per heartbeat tick; if the config hot-reloads between tick start and the second `sendDurableMessageBatch` call, the heartbeat-ok and main reply could in theory carry slightly different snapshots.
  - Mitigation: identity is resolved once at the top of `runHeartbeatOnce` and reused for both calls in the same tick, so the heartbeat-ok and main reply always carry the identical snapshot. A subsequent tick will pick up any hot-reloaded config naturally.
target.author: rohang2005

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
